lead-generation.title: AI Lead Generation & Pipeline
lead-generation.tagline: Every inbound lead. Qualified. In 30 seconds.
lead-generation.summary: An autonomous lead engine. Captures every inbound lead in real time, scores it 0–100 against your ICP, replies in your brand voice, follows up on a configurable cadence, and books qualified meetings directly into your sales team's calendar. Octio's most-deployed product.
lead-generation.details: We unify intake from your website forms, Google Ads, Meta lead-gen forms, LinkedIn ads + DMs, inbound email, and WhatsApp Business — every lead surfaces in one pipeline within seconds. Each lead is scored across three signal layers: firmographic fit (industry, size, geography from Clearbit + Apollo enrichment, weighted 30%), behavioural signals (pages visited, content engaged with, response patterns, weighted 50%), and intent signals (Bombora topic surge, G2 category research, hiring patterns, weighted 20%). Score thresholds are calibrated to your closed-won data. Anything below 40 goes to nurture; 40–70 gets longer qualification; 70+ goes straight to your sales team with a 'respond now' tag. The first reply to every lead lands within 30 seconds, in a voice we tune to match your past outbound and brand. Every message includes lead-specific context — the page they came from, what they downloaded, an industry signal — never a template. When a qualified lead is ready to book, the AI proposes 3 slots from the right team member's actual calendar (live read from Google Calendar / Outlook), books the meeting, sends a Meet link, and updates your CRM. Every meeting booked includes a structured pre-call brief delivered 30 minutes before the call.
lead-generation.typical_timeline: 48 hours from kickoff to live agent
lead-generation.pricing_range: Starter R8 500/month (up to 200 leads, single channel) → Growth R18 500/month (up to 1 000 leads, multi-channel) → Enterprise custom
ideal_for: B2B businesses where speed-to-lead determines deal outcomes (most do — 74% of B2B companies miss the 5-minute response window)
Sales teams overwhelmed by inbound from multiple channels with no unified pipeline
Marketing teams paying for ads but losing 60-80% of leads to slow follow-up
Founders who need outbound qualification without hiring a full SDR team yet
pain_points: We pay for leads then lose them because nobody replies fast enough
Senior reps are buried in unqualified inbound and can't get to real prospects
Inbound is scattered across 5+ tools and we have no unified pipeline view
We've tried generic AI chatbots and they sound like ChatGPT — leads disengage
social_proof: A B2B SaaS company cut their average response time from 4 hours to 30 seconds — 3× more qualified meetings booked per week within 90 days
A professional services firm filtered 80% of inbound out of their senior partners' inboxes; partners only saw genuine prospects
A logistics company unified intake from 6 channels into a single pipeline — pipeline visibility went from days to real-time

common_objections.objection: Our leads are too specialised — generic AI won't qualify them properly
common_objections.response: That's why we tune the scoring to your closed-won data during onboarding, not against a generic ICP template. The first 30 days is calibration — we adjust the weighting against your real data. After that the AI is making the same judgement calls your senior SDR would, just 24/7 without the bottleneck.

common_objections.objection: Our brand voice is critical — AI replies will sound generic
common_objections.response: Generic AI replies do kill conversion. We onboard with 30+ examples of your past outbound + sales replies + landing copy, extract your tone and structural patterns, and tune the model against your actual voice. The first month is a feedback loop — flag anything that doesn't sound like you and the model adapts. Most clients can't tell our drafts from human-written ones by week 4.

common_objections.objection: We already use HubSpot / Salesforce — what's the difference?
common_objections.response: We integrate with both. HubSpot's native lead scoring is rules-based and doesn't actually qualify against intent. We add the layer on top: real-time AI scoring, personalised first-touch in your voice, calendar booking, and pre-call briefs — feeding back into your existing CRM as the source of truth.

call_prep_questions: What CRM are you currently using?
What channels are most of your inbound leads coming from?
What's your current average response time to a new inbound lead?
How is sales currently handling lead qualification — manual rep review, scoring rules, or none?
Roughly how many leads per month across all channels?

voice-chat.title: Voice & Chat Agents
voice-chat.tagline: Pick up every call. Book every appointment. Never miss another inbound.
voice-chat.summary: 24/7 AI receptionists across phone, web chat, and WhatsApp. Natural-sounding, brand-tuned, and indistinguishable from a real receptionist for the first minute of conversation. Books appointments, qualifies callers, takes detailed messages, and warm-transfers to your team when it matters.
voice-chat.details: Most businesses miss 62% of their inbound calls — only 37.8% are actually answered, per industry data. Voicemail doesn't fix it: 85% of callers don't leave one, and of those who do, less than 20% get called back in time. Each unanswered call costs an estimated $200–$1,200 depending on industry. Octio's voice agents fix this by picking up sub-1-second every call, every time. The agent uses neural TTS tuned to your brand voice, handles mid-call interruptions, accents, background noise, and rapid speech. Multilingual on Enterprise: English, Afrikaans, Zulu, Xhosa, Sesotho. The same agent answers your phone, your website's live chat widget, and your WhatsApp Business number — context is preserved across channels. It runs your defined qualification flow conversationally (not as a script), reads availability live from Google Calendar / Outlook, books appointments with the right team member based on routing logic (territory, language, expertise area), and warm-transfers to a human when defined triggers fire (price >R50K, frustrated caller, legal questions, anything outside the trained scope).
voice-chat.typical_timeline: 5–7 days from kickoff to live (includes voice tuning + escalation rules)
voice-chat.pricing_range: Voice Starter R6 500/month (500 calls, single line) → Voice + Chat R12 500/month (2 000 conversations) → Enterprise custom
ideal_for: Practices and clinics (dental, medical, veterinary) overwhelmed at peak hours and missing after-hours calls
Professional services firms whose senior partners spend 2+ hours/day on intake calls
Real estate agencies losing leads to competitors who answer first
Any business where missed calls directly cost revenue
pain_points: Front desk gets buried in calls during peak hours; reception suffers
After-hours calls go to voicemail and never get returned
Senior staff waste hours on intake calls before deciding if a lead is worth their time
Receptionist turnover is high; quality is inconsistent
social_proof: A dental practice group reduced after-hours missed calls from 40% to zero — emergency cases now triaged and booked directly into the morning's schedule
A legal firm replaced manual intake interviews — partners now only handle pre-qualified cases, freeing 2 hours a day per partner
A real estate agency captured leads while agents were showing properties — agent walks into the next showing already briefed on the next caller's needs

common_objections.objection: Won't callers know they're talking to AI and hang up?
common_objections.response: Most don't notice for the first 60 seconds. When they ask directly, the agent is upfront — 'I'm an AI assistant for [Company], here to help with bookings and quick questions.' We've found being honest about the AI doesn't kill the conversation; trying to hide it does. You can also configure the agent to disclose by default if your industry requires it.

common_objections.objection: What about complex questions or angry callers?
common_objections.response: Defined escalation triggers warm-transfer to a human in seconds — you set the rules. Pricing above a threshold, mention of a competitor, sentiment turning negative, anything legal — all hand off automatically. The AI stays on the line during transfer to brief your human, so the caller doesn't repeat themselves.

common_objections.objection: We have a switchboard / IVR already — why replace it?
common_objections.response: Most IVRs are decision trees that frustrate callers. The agent is conversational — it listens, understands intent, and acts. We can run it alongside your existing system as an after-hours fallback first if you want to test before fully replacing.

call_prep_questions: How many inbound calls do you typically receive per day / per week?
What % currently go to voicemail or unanswered?
What does a typical inbound call look like — booking, FAQ, support, sales?
Do you need multilingual support (Afrikaans, Zulu, Xhosa)?
What's the existing phone setup — VoIP provider, call routing rules?

social-media.title: AI Social Media Manager
social-media.tagline: Industry-specific content in your voice — drafted, scheduled, published, measured.
social-media.summary: Auto-generates 40+ posts per month across LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, X, and your blog — in your brand voice, with AI-generated visuals, on smart timing tuned to your audience. You approve. The AI does the rest.
social-media.details: Most company social accounts post for 8 weeks then go silent. Not because content marketing doesn't work — it does — but because curating, drafting, designing, and shipping every week is a real job nobody has time for. The Social Media Manager replaces that work. Every Monday, the system scans 50–100 industry sources you nominated: news sites, competitor accounts, trending hashtags in your niche, recent earnings calls of public companies you sell to, and discussions on relevant subreddits / X communities / LinkedIn comments. It ranks what's surfacing by recency × relevance × novelty, producing a weekly content brief of 8–12 angles to post about. From there, drafts arrive in your voice (trained on 30–50 of your past posts during onboarding), with on-brand AI-generated visuals (carousels for LinkedIn, lifestyle shots for Instagram, video scripts for TikTok), scheduled to your audience's specific peak engagement windows. You approve, edit, or skip. After 30–60 days most clients move to hybrid auto-publish — trusted formats auto-post, opinion pieces still get manual review.
social-media.typical_timeline: 10–14 days from kickoff to first scheduled post (voice tuning takes the time)
social-media.pricing_range: Single Channel R4 500/month → Multi-Channel R9 500/month → Agency custom
ideal_for: B2B firms whose senior team should be doing thought leadership but has no bandwidth
Specialised practices (dentistry, accounting, legal) needing industry-specific weekly content
Solo founders building a personal brand without 10 hours a week to spend writing
Marketing teams of 1 who can't keep up with content + ads + email
pain_points: We started a LinkedIn / Instagram strategy and went silent in month 3 because nobody had time
Our content sounds like every other generic AI tool — readers can spot it
We have great ideas in our heads but they never become posts
We need more content output than our team can sustain
social_proof: A dentistry posting weekly oral-health tips: 4 LinkedIn + 3 Instagram per week, on-brand. Engagement up 8× in 90 days
An accounting firm where partners hated writing: daily LinkedIn posts in each partner's voice. Two enterprise leads attributed directly to LinkedIn content
A B2B SaaS marketer of 1 who freed up for higher-value work — AI handled blog + social, marketer focused on campaigns and partnerships

common_objections.objection: Generic AI content is recognisable and damages our brand
common_objections.response: We don't do generic AI content. Onboarding spends 30+ examples of your past posts plus competitor examples you admire — the AI extracts your tone, structural patterns, and signature CTAs. The first month is feedback-driven; you flag anything that doesn't sound like you. Most clients can't pick AI posts from human-written ones by week 4.

common_objections.objection: We need to approve everything — won't that just shift the bottleneck?
common_objections.response: It's a bulk review, not a per-post review. You approve a week's worth in 5–10 minutes vs spending hours writing them. Edit-in-place is in the dashboard. After 30 days most clients move to hybrid: trusted formats (e.g. weekly stat posts) auto-publish, opinion pieces stay manual. You control the rules.

call_prep_questions: Which channels matter most for your audience (LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, blog)?
Do you have past content / brand guidelines we can use to tune voice?
Who is your target audience and what topics do they care about?
What's your current posting cadence and engagement baseline?

newsletter.title: The Newsletter Engine
newsletter.tagline: Weekly newsletter. In your voice. On autopilot.
newsletter.summary: Tell us your niche and audience. The Newsletter Engine scans 50–100 industry sources weekly, drafts a newsletter in your voice with your perspective, generates the artwork, and ships it through your existing email tool (Mailchimp, Beehiiv, Substack, ConvertKit). You approve. It runs forever.
newsletter.details: Newsletter is the highest-ROI marketing channel in 2026 — average open rates are 41% across the industry — but only if it actually goes out. Most newsletters started this year will stop publishing within 6 months because curating + writing + designing + shipping every week is a job nobody actually has time for. The engine replaces that job. Each week the system scans 50–100 sources: news sites, blog feeds, podcast transcripts, YouTube channel uploads, Twitter / LinkedIn discussions, and any custom sources you give us (SEC filings, regulatory updates, internal company blogs). It deduplicates, filters out PR fluff, and ranks what's left by recency × source quality × relevance to your audience. Drafts are written in your voice with your perspective — extracted from your past writing during onboarding — not flat summaries. Each issue gets a custom AI-generated header image, your colour palette, your logo, and inbox-friendly formatting. Engagement data (opens, click-throughs by section, reply rate, unsubscribes, forwards) feeds back into next week's content selection — within 6–8 issues the brief looks like an editor who's been with you for years.
newsletter.typical_timeline: 10–14 days from kickoff to first issue
newsletter.pricing_range: Solo R3 500/month (bi-weekly) → Business R6 500/month (weekly + branded design) → Enterprise custom
ideal_for: Independent consultants wanting to stay top-of-mind with past clients
Niche SaaS founders building thought leadership without hiring a content team
Personal brands / creators who want the audience without writing long-form weekly
Companies needing newsletter as a top-of-funnel channel they can sustain
pain_points: We started a newsletter, sent 3 issues, then it died because nobody had bandwidth
Our list is going cold because we haven't sent anything in months
We want a newsletter but have no idea what to write about every week
Our writing voice is hard to replicate — generic AI summaries don't sound like us
social_proof: A consultant's bi-weekly newsletter on industry trends: 35% open rate, 2 reactivated clients within 90 days
A niche SaaS founder's weekly newsletter ranking #1 in its niche; 5× newsletter-driven sign-ups in 6 months
A creator's weekly 'best of' newsletter summarising their YouTube + LinkedIn output — audience grew 4× in a year

common_objections.objection: AI-generated newsletter content sounds like flat summaries
common_objections.response: We don't write summaries. The AI extracts your perspective from your past writing — your hot takes, recurring themes, the angles you reach for. Every story in every issue gets framed through that lens. Readers feel like they're hearing from you, not a content mill. The proof is in the first 2-3 issues; if they don't sound like you, we keep tuning.

common_objections.objection: What happens if I want to skip a week or add my own content?
common_objections.response: Skip in the dashboard — toggle off and the AI resumes the next cycle. Your own content drops in as a custom section and the AI builds the issue around it. You're never locked into the AI's content calendar; the engine works with you.

call_prep_questions: What's your topic / niche and who reads it?
What's your unique angle or perspective in this space?
What email tool are you using (or thinking of) — Mailchimp, ConvertKit, Beehiiv, Substack?
What cadence — weekly, bi-weekly, monthly?
Do you have 2–3 newsletters you admire we should reference for tone?

agentic-app-dev.title: Agentic Web & App Development
agentic-app-dev.tagline: Production apps in weeks, not quarters. At a quarter of the cost.
agentic-app-dev.summary: Custom web and mobile applications built using AI agents to do the boilerplate (70% of any build) and senior human developers to do the judgement (30% that matters). Agency-quality output, fraction of agency cost and timeline. You own the code outright.
agentic-app-dev.details: Traditional agency timelines are dominated by repetitive work — auth flows, CRUD endpoints, form validation, basic UI components, deployment plumbing. None of it is intellectually challenging; all of it costs time. We use AI agents to produce these layers in parallel from the architecture spec we agree at kickoff. A typical web app that an agency would scope at 12–16 weeks ships from us in 3–6 weeks. Senior developer time is reserved for what AI can't be trusted with: data model decisions, edge-case logic, security review, product judgement. Stack defaults: Next.js 15 + Postgres + Vercel for web, React Native + Expo for mobile, with shared TypeScript types between them. Where a project genuinely needs native (iOS/Android-only or heavy device integration), we go native — Swift, Kotlin. We pick stacks based on what's easiest for any developer to maintain after handover, not what's exciting to build with. Every line is reviewed (or rewritten) by a senior developer with 8+ years of production experience before you see it. You see a working version every week. 30 days of post-launch support included.
agentic-app-dev.typical_timeline: MVP Sprint: 2–3 weeks → Production Build: 6–10 weeks → Long-term Partner: ongoing retainer
agentic-app-dev.pricing_range: MVP Sprint from R85 000 → Production Build from R220 000 → Long-term Partner custom retainer
ideal_for: Founders validating an idea who need a real working MVP fast on a tight budget
Operations teams replacing 4 spreadsheets with a custom internal tool
Existing apps needing a rewrite (legacy stack, performance issues) without R1.5M+ quotes
Companies tired of agency hand-offs to junior developers and project managers
pain_points: Agency quotes are R600K–R1.5M for what feels like a basic web app
Existing platform is slow, hard to maintain, and rebuild quotes feel insane
We need to ship something to validate before sinking 6 months of runway into it
Past agency engagements gave us code we couldn't maintain after handover
social_proof: A B2B SaaS founder's MVP shipped in 3 weeks for R85K — validated, raised seed in month 2
An ops team's spreadsheet replacement reclaimed ~80 hours/month of manual work
A native rewrite quoted at R1.5M and 6 months done in React Native + AI scaffolding for R280K in 7 weeks

common_objections.objection: AI-generated code is unmaintainable — we'll be locked in
common_objections.response: We don't ship AI slop. Every line is reviewed by a senior developer, follows standard patterns, has tests, and is documented. Stacks are boring and proven (Next.js, React Native, Postgres) so any developer in the world can pick up where we left off. You get the source, deployment access, all credentials. No lock-in, no license fees.

common_objections.objection: How is this different from a normal agency?
common_objections.response: Most agencies sell senior names then staff your project with juniors. We don't have a junior layer — AI does the volume work, seniors do the judgement. That's why our timelines compress 3–4×: we skip the round-trips between juniors and seniors that consume agency hours.

common_objections.objection: Can you take over an existing codebase or just build new?
common_objections.response: Yes — modernising legacy code is exactly the work AI accelerates the most. Most of legacy maintenance is repetitive refactoring; the AI handles that and our seniors focus on architectural decisions. We've taken over codebases from agencies, freelancers, and in-house teams and shipped them to production.

call_prep_questions: What are you building / what does success look like in 6 months?
Who are the users — external customers, internal staff, or both?
Is there an existing codebase to extend / replace, or is this greenfield?
What's the absolute deadline if any — funding round, customer commitment, season?
What's your in-house technical capacity for maintenance after handover?

custom-workflows.title: Custom Agentic Workflows
custom-workflows.tagline: Replace your team's busywork with workflows that think.
custom-workflows.summary: Connect your disjointed tools (CRM, calendar, support, project, finance) into a unified AI brain. Custom workflows route, decide, summarise, escalate, and act on their own — replacing the human glue that ops teams spend 4–6 hours a day on.
custom-workflows.details: Your CRM, calendar, support tool, project tracker, and finance system all do their jobs. They just don't talk to each other. So your team plays human glue — copy-pasting, status-updating, reminding, escalating. We've seen ops teams spend 4–6 hours a day on this work; that's 25% of payroll going to something AI does in seconds and gets right more often than humans do. We start with a 1-week audit: we sit with your ops, sales, and support teams and map the real flow — where work originates, where it stalls, who picks it up, what they do, where they hand it off, what gets dropped. Output is a current-state diagram and a target-state diagram side by side. We then build the target state with 100+ pre-built integrations (Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, Slack, Teams, Notion, Asana, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, Stripe, Xero, Sage, Zendesk, Intercom, Calendly + many more) plus custom integrations for anything bespoke. AI agents are inserted at decision points: 'is this support ticket urgent?' (classifies), 'which sales rep should this lead go to?' (territory + capacity + expertise), 'should we escalate this invoice?' (history + value + customer health). Every decision is auditable — the agent logs its reasoning. 30-day pilot side-by-side before cutover.
custom-workflows.pricing_range: Workflow Audit R35 000 (one-off) → Build + Pilot from R150 000 → Ongoing Operations from R25 000/month
ideal_for: Ops-heavy companies whose teams spend hours per day on coordination work
Businesses where data is right but lives in 5+ tools that don't sync
Teams whose handoffs (sales→onboarding, support→engineering) routinely lose context
Mid-market companies wanting AI value without buying a new platform
pain_points: Our ops team spends 4 hours a day on Slack updates, CRM entry, and follow-up emails
Things fall through the cracks between sales and onboarding constantly
We have 6 tools and management still can't get a unified view
We tried Zapier / Make but they can't handle the decisions we need automated
social_proof: A professional services firm replaced 4-hour/day post-call workflow with an AI workflow handling 90% of it; ops manager freed up for higher-leverage work
An e-commerce support team's tickets — AI triages, escalates VIPs, drafts responses; 35% faster routing
A real estate agency: listing updates that took 6 platforms now propagate in minutes from a single source of truth

common_objections.objection: We use Zapier — why do we need this?
common_objections.response: Zapier moves data between systems but can't make decisions. That's where workflows fall apart — every interesting decision still needs a human. We insert AI agents at exactly the points that need judgement: 'is this urgent?', 'who should handle this?', 'should we escalate?'. Zapier handles the plumbing; we handle the brain.

common_objections.objection: What if my tool isn't on your integration list?
common_objections.response: Custom integration is part of the work. We've connected bespoke ERPs, legacy databases, even SOAP APIs. If it has any kind of programmatic interface, we can connect it.

common_objections.objection: How do we know it actually saved time?
common_objections.response: Every workflow we build comes with a real-time dashboard: time in stage, where bottlenecks are, % of decisions the AI handled vs escalated, error rates. We define metrics during the audit (usually hours saved per week, error rate reduction, or specific business outcomes) and measure them monthly.

call_prep_questions: What's the most painful manual workflow your ops team handles right now?
How many tools is your team using day-to-day across CRM, support, projects, finance?
Where do handoffs between departments break down most often?
What's the ideal ratio of automated-to-manual work for your business?
Have you tried Zapier / Make / similar — what worked, what didn't?

corporate-advisory.title: Corporate AI Advisory & Adoption
corporate-advisory.tagline: From 'we should try AI' to AI generating real revenue — in 90 days.
corporate-advisory.summary: AI strategy + governance + execution for mid-market and enterprise. We assess readiness, build POPIA / GDPR / ISO 42001 governance, prioritise high-ROI use cases against your specific business, train your staff role-by-role, and pilot the top 2–3 use cases ourselves so you have wins on the board.
corporate-advisory.details: 80% of AI projects fail to deliver business value (RAND, 2025). 88% of agent pilots never make it to production. Average abandoned AI initiative cost: $7.2M before being shelved. The pattern is consistent: chosen because of vendor pitch (not internal pain), scoped wider than feasible, no measurable success criteria, no executive sponsor, no plan for production handover. We start with a 2-week readiness assessment covering four dimensions: data (what you have, where it lives, what you can legally use it for), infrastructure (cloud maturity, identity, integration capability), processes (which workflows are AI-ready vs need cleaning up first), and people (skill levels by role, current adoption patterns, change-management capacity). Output: written readiness report with 5–10 specific 90-day quick wins identified. Governance framework follows: POPIA + GDPR + ISO 42001 (the 2026 gold standard — 83% of Fortune 500 procurement teams plan to require alignment by 2027; certified companies already getting 15–25% lower AI liability insurance premiums). We then prioritise use cases against impact / effort / risk, building a sequenced 12-month roadmap. Critical: we don't just advise — the Adoption Programme includes piloting the top 2–3 use cases ourselves so you have measurable wins, not a glossy PowerPoint. Role-specific training (marketing, ops, finance, support each get separate hands-on programmes) measured at 30/60/90 days for actual adoption, not attendance.
stats: 15-25% lower AI liability premiums when ISO 42001-aligned
corporate-advisory.typical_timeline: Readiness Audit: 2–3 weeks → Adoption Programme: 90 days (audit + governance + roadmap + first pilots) → Embedded AI Office: ongoing
corporate-advisory.pricing_range: Readiness Audit R125 000 (one-off, credited toward programme) → Adoption Programme from R450 000 (90 days) → Embedded AI Office from R65 000/month
ideal_for: Mid-market and enterprise (typically 50+ employees, R50M+ revenue) — below that our products usually deliver more value than advisory
Boards that need an AI strategy but are wary of vendor pitches
Compliance-sensitive industries (financial services, healthcare, professional services) where governance comes first
CEOs who've watched 80% of AI projects fail and want a different approach
pain_points: We have 'AI strategy' on the board agenda but no clear plan to execute
Our last AI vendor sold us tools nobody adopted; we sunk R1M for no return
Compliance team is blocking AI rollouts and we can't unstick it
Staff are using ChatGPT with customer data and we have no policy
social_proof: A bank built private AI infrastructure + governance — 4 productive use cases live in 90 days, zero data incidents
A healthcare practice group whose staff feared AI adoption — trained + compliant tooling saved 30% of admin time across 12 practices
A mid-market manufacturer's roadmap identified 3 high-ROI use cases (procurement, quality, scheduling); ~R4M savings projected year 1

common_objections.objection: Pure advisory firms hand you a roadmap and disappear — how is this different?
common_objections.response: The Adoption Programme includes piloting the first 2–3 use cases ourselves. We build them, run them, measure them, and hand off only after they've delivered measurable value. Most pure-advisory firms can't or won't do that. The wins on the board create the political capital your team needs to expand AI internally without us.

common_objections.objection: Compliance is our biggest blocker — can you actually navigate POPIA + ISO 42001?
common_objections.response: POPIA + GDPR + ISO 42001 (and EU AI Act where relevant) are baked into the governance framework from day one. Every AI vendor and tool we recommend is vetted against these standards. We've worked with financial services and healthcare clients where compliance was the gating concern, and got them to live deployments inside 90 days.

common_objections.objection: Our staff are very junior on AI — generic ChatGPT training didn't stick
common_objections.response: Generic training never sticks because it doesn't translate to specific work. We do role-specific programmes — marketing learns AI for content + analysis + competitor research; ops learns AI for routing + summarisation + reporting; finance learns AI for variance analysis + audit prep. We measure adoption at 30/60/90 days, not attendance — and update the programme if uptake stalls.

common_objections.objection: Will you recommend tools we have to buy?
common_objections.response: Sometimes — but no kickback arrangements with any vendor. Recommendations are based purely on what fits your business. Plenty of recommendations end up being 'use what you already have, just differently'.

call_prep_questions: What size is your organisation (employees, revenue) and what industry?
What's currently driving the AI conversation — board pressure, competitive threat, regulation, customer demand?
What past AI initiatives have you tried? What worked, what didn't?
Where is your compliance posture currently (POPIA / GDPR / sector-specific)?
Who are the executive sponsors and what's their tolerance for measurable risk vs measurable upside?

general.title: About Octio
general.summary: Octio is a pure-play AI company. We build, deploy, and train autonomous AI agents that run your marketing, manage your leads, automate your back-office, and develop your custom software at unprecedented speeds. Based in Pretoria, South Africa.
general.details: Octio is structured around four autonomous AI products (Lead Generation, Voice & Chat, Social Media Manager, Newsletter Engine) and three services (Agentic App Development, Custom Workflows, Corporate AI Advisory). Our differentiator is that we are our own Patient Zero — every product we sell powers Octio's own business operations first. We don't sell theories; we sell tested agentic systems. Senior-led, no junior layer, BYOK-friendly pricing where it makes sense. We take on a limited number of advisory engagements at any time so each client gets genuine senior attention.

company_info.engagement_capacity: 3–4 active advisory engagements at any time
company_info.specialisation: Pure-play AI — products, services, and adoption

pricing.title: Octio Pricing
pricing.summary: Octio combines productised SaaS-style pricing for autonomous AI products with project-based engagements for custom services. Discovery call required to scope correctly.

products_subscription.Newsletter Engine: Solo R3 500/mo · Business R6 500/mo · Enterprise custom

pricing_philosophy: BYOK (bring your own keys) where it makes sense — flexible, transparent, scales with your usage
Audits credit toward implementation projects — no double-charging
Senior-led, fixed scope where possible — no hourly-billing surprises
Discovery call required to scope accurately — we don't quote without understanding the work

process.title: How Octio Engagements Work
process.summary: For autonomous products: 3-step deployment (Define Rules → Connect Systems → Go Autonomous). For custom builds: 6-step delivery (Talk → Design → Prototype → Review → Build → Go Live).
process.details: Both flows start with a discovery call. For products, we typically go from kickoff to live in 5–14 days depending on the product. For custom services, project sprints with weekly demos give you a working version every 5 working days — feedback loops are 24-hour, not 2-week.

products: 1. Define your rules — business domain, audience, brand voice (kickoff call)
2. Connect your systems — calendars, CRMs, social accounts via secure portal
3. Go autonomous — AI agent live, you approve and monitor
custom_builds: 1. Let's talk — discovery call to understand vision, challenges, goals
3. Let's prototype — working prototype to validate before full build
5. Let's build — full development with rigorous testing
6. Let's go live — deployment + handover + 30-day support

ownership_principles: You own all source code and infrastructure outright
Documentation + handover support included on every project
Standard, boring stacks so any developer can take over
30-day post-launch support included; ongoing retainer optional
